tldr:
• 🔴 ActionESMA DLT Pilot Regime consultation CLOSED TODAY (Jun 15) — Montis response submitted; ESMA enters collation phase, final framework guidance expected Q3 2026. The submission window that shaped EU DLT CSD infrastructure is now locked.
  • 🟠 WatchBoE MPC decision Thursday Jun 19 — 4 days. +25bp → 5.50% priced at 80%. UK CPI May (Tue Jun 17) is the last key input before the decision; wage growth remains the swing factor.
  • 🟠 WatchEuronext Securities CSSF DLT CSD application now under formal assessment. Filed Jun 12; CSSF Q4 2026 decision expected. Montis and Euronext now in parallel assessment in Luxembourg — differentiation on DLT-nativeness becomes the decisive pitch.
montis:
• 🔴 ActionEuronext Securities files CSSF DLT CSD pilot application — direct rival enters LU home market (Jun 12) Euronext Securities, operator of six European CSDs, has filed a formal DLT Pilot Regime application with the CSSF Luxembourg for authorisation as a DLT Central Securities Depository, targeting tokenised fund units and structured-product settlement. Expected CSSF decision: Q4 2026. 💡 Montis angle: The most direct competitive threat to date — a Tier-1 CSD incumbent with existing EU relationships and full regulatory track record is applying in Montis's home jurisdiction. Accelerates the need to reach CSSF authorisation before Euronext completes its process; competitive differentiation on DLT-nativeness and Pontes-readiness becomes the pitch. ↗ Euronext Securities · June 12
  • 🔴 ActionPaxos = 1st SEC-registered blockchain clearing agency & CSD — the post-DTCC era opens (Jun 4) The SEC registered Paxos Securities Settlement Company (PSSC) as a clearing agency under Section 17A — the first and only blockchain-native firm approved as a registered CSD in the US (18-month temporary registration, 7 years of SEC engagement). PSSC ran a settlement pilot with major institutions. 💡 Montis angle: A compliant, blockchain-native CSD competitor to DTCC now exists legally inside a major regulatory perimeter — the precedent every European supervisor and counterparty will reference. Strengthens the authorisation narrative and the institutional sales case. ↗ Paxos / Federal Register · June 4
  • 🔴 ActionEuroclear Sweden + Finland → unified "One Nordic" CSD · TCS BaNCS + T2S migration (Jun 4) TCS will migrate Euroclear Sweden and Finland to a unified Nordic post-trade platform (TCS BaNCS for Market Infrastructure + Quartz), cloud-ready and aligned to the ECB's TARGET2-Securities (T2S) standards — two sovereign markets under one scalable CSD. 💡 Montis angle: Legacy CSD consolidation around T2S accelerates demand for DLT-native alternatives that can plug into T2S and ECB Pontes. The incumbents are spending years and fortunes on modernisation Montis can offer natively. ↗ TCS / PostTrade360 · June 4
  • 🔴 ActionClearBank Europe Digital Asset Rails live — EURC + SEPA Instant 24/7, MiCA-compliant (Jun 2) ClearBank Europe launched live Digital Asset Rails: institutions convert fiat → EURC (Circle MiCA stablecoin) → transfer cross-border → redeem fiat via SEPA Instant 24/7. First Dutch bank with a MiCA notification; USDC support due H2 2026. 💡 Montis angle: The first operational, regulated cash-leg for EU institutional settlement — exactly the programmable cash layer that runs alongside a DLT securities platform. A potential interoperability partner, not just a market signal. ↗ ClearBank · June 2
  • 🟠 WatchBIS Project Agorá — 7 central banks deliver wholesale-CBDC + tokenised-deposit unified ledger (May 28) The BIS and seven central banks (BoE, NY Fed, Banque de France/Eurosystem, BoJ, BoK, Banco de México, SNB) delivered a working prototype combining tokenised commercial-bank deposits and wholesale CBDC reserves on one programmable ledger with atomic, cross-border, multi-currency settlement. Advancing to real-value testing. 💡 Montis angle: This is the wholesale-CBDC cash-leg infrastructure that would underpin institutional DLT settlement. The direction of travel for the settlement asset Montis securities will ultimately settle against. ↗ PostTrade360 · May 28
  • 🟠 WatchDTCC + Stellar — multi-chain tokenisation of Treasuries, ETFs & equities (May 27) DTCC will connect its tokenisation service to the Stellar blockchain as part of a multi-chain strategy. Eligible assets: Russell 1000 equities, ETFs, US Treasuries; full lifecycle support with the same investor protections as conventional securities. H1 2027 launch target. 💡 Montis angle: The US CSD incumbent is going on-chain. A direct pressure point on European DLT infrastructure players to reach production first within the EU regulatory framework. ↗ PostTrade360 · May 27
dlt:
• 🟠 WatchCiti: tokenised securities to reach $5.5 trillion by 2030 — "Wall Street On-Chain" (Jun 1) Citi's landmark "Tokenization 2030" report projects the tokenised-securities market growing from $17bn today to $5.5 trillion by 2030 (base case). By 2030: 10% of the US short-term Treasury market and 3% of listed equities could be tokenised; stablecoins → $1.9T. DTCC, Nasdaq and ICE are all building on-chain. 💡 Montis angle: Validates the institutional addressable market with a number sell-side and buy-side both cite. Useful anchor for investor and client conversations about the size of the prize. ↗ CoinDesk · June 1
  • 🟠 WatchReconciliation — DLT's "unsung power tool" and structural cost advantage (Jun 5) Vinod Jain (PostTrade360) argues reconciliation is DLT's underestimated edge: a shared "golden source" between counterparties eliminates bilateral reconciliation — one of the most expensive line items in legacy CSD back-office. The argument goes beyond T+0 or programmability and challenges the whole economics of post-trade. 💡 Montis angle: The operational-cost story is a powerful institutional sales vector — it reframes DLT from "faster settlement" to "structurally cheaper to run." Worth building into the commercial pitch. ↗ PostTrade360 · June 5
  • ⚪ ContextSurvey: 50% of asset managers have started tokenisation projects (Apex Group, May 28) Apex Group survey: 50% of asset managers have deployed tokenisation; 45% have tokenised private-market assets; 42% cite expanding investor access as the primary driver. Only 7% feel "fully confident" in technical capabilities. 💡 Montis angle: Demand side is ready; infrastructure and expertise are the bottleneck — precisely the supply-side gap a regulated DLT market infrastructure fills. ↗ PostTrade360 · May 28
  • ⚪ ContextAI + supervision — Anthropic warns models "may soon be too powerful to control" (Jun 7) Anthropic issued a rare public warning that its models may soon exceed human ability to control or correct them. (Only AI item kept — relevance is governance/supervision of AI in regulated finance, not consumer tech.) 💡 Montis angle: AI governance is moving up the regulator agenda (ESMA, EBA). Any AI used in post-trade ops, surveillance or onboarding will face the same model-risk and explainability expectations — design for auditability now. ↗ BuildFastWithAI · June 7

sum:
📅 Week Jun 8–12 closed
S&P −0.8% week → 7,325 · Bund 10Y 3.05% · Brent $91.50 · ECB 2.25% digested
🏦 BoE Jun 19 — 4 days
+25bp → 5.50% priced at 80% · UK CPI Tue 17 key input · wages swing factor
📋 ESMA DLT consultation closed
Closed today Jun 15 · Montis response in · Q3 2026 ESMA framework analysis
🎯 Polymarket odds of the day
BoE Jun 19 +25bp: 80% · US-Iran MoU Jun: 62% · Fed Sep cut: 38%
😐 Quiet Monday open · BoE Jun 19 in focus · ESMA consultation closed — Monday June 15. Markets open quietly after a week that saw the ECB deliver +25bp (→ 2.25%), S&P close the week −0.8% → 7,325, and Brent ease to $91.50 on Hormuz de-escalation signals. The ESMA DLT Pilot Regime consultation closed today — submissions now locked, Q3 framework guidance the next catalyst. BoE MPC June 19 (+25bp → 5.50% at 80%) is the week's key event; UK CPI May (Tuesday) is the swing input. Macro footnote: Bund 10Y holding 3.05% — Lagarde Sep 2nd hike priced at 65%+.
geo:
⚠️ Iran · MoU Status
Brent stable $91.50 — MoU framework unsigned but no new strikes overnight
No new US or Iranian strike activity overnight Jun 14–15. Both sides are holding the MoU framework open; the 60-day window is running. Brent steady at $91.50; WTI $88.80. Hormuz remains at elevated alert but passage is open. Next flashpoint: US Congress Iran sanctions vote expected this week.
↗ Reuters · June 15
🏦 BoE · Pre-decision Week
BoE Jun 19 four days away — UK CPI Tue key · Gilts steady at 4.84%
Market consensus: BoE MPC delivers +25bp → 5.50% on Thursday Jun 19 (80% on Polymarket). Gilt 10Y holding at 4.84% overnight. UK CPI May (Tuesday Jun 17) is the decisive data point — a hot print above 3.5% YoY could re-open debate on a larger move. BoE speakers expected Mon/Tue.
↗ Bank of England · June 15
📋 ESMA · Regulatory
ESMA DLT Pilot Regime consultation closed today — Q3 framework locked
The ESMA consultation on the DLT Pilot Regime extension officially closed today Jun 15. All respondents — including Euronext Securities (CSSF applicant, Jun 12) — have submitted. ESMA now enters a multi-week collation and analysis phase; Q3 2026 guidance will set the definitive EU DLT CSD framework through 2027.
↗ ESMA · June 15
